description: §7.6 — two file-level `${ }` blocks each declaring `const dup` collide in the shared file scope, firing E-SCOPE-010 (duplicate binding in file scope). File-level `${ }` blocks do NOT introduce isolated scopes (§7.6), so the second declaration re-declares a name already in scope.
rationale: §7.6: re-declaring a name already declared at file scope in a later file-level `${ }` block SHALL be a compile error (E-SCOPE-010: duplicate binding in file scope). Two `${ }` blocks both binding `dup` trip exactly this.
